exemplar.title: How strongly do varying pH levels of soda beverages (4.27, 3.24, 3.22, 3.10, 2.37) influence the demineralization of calcium carbonate (CaCO3) in chicken eggshells (Gallus gallus domesticus), as measured by mass loss over a 24-hour period?

detailedExemplarData.title: How strongly do varying pH levels of soda beverages (4.27, 3.24, 3.22, 3.10, 2.37) influence the demineralization of calcium carbonate (CaCO3) in chicken eggshells (Gallus gallus domesticus), as measured by mass loss over a 24-hour period?

subject.title: Biology
subject.description:
**IB Biology** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **four interconnected themes: Unity and Diversity, Form and Function, Interaction and Interdependence, and Continuity and Change**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, scientific inquiry, data analysis, and practical investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice and data-based questions | 1 hour 20 min | 2 hours | 36% |
| **Paper 2** | Short and extended response questions | 1 hour 30 min | 2 hours 30 min | 44% |
| **Scientific Investigation** | Individual research project | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: The research question is clearly articulated within a specific context linking soda acidity, dental health, and eggshell CaCOâ as a model.
Methodological considerations (IV, DV, controls, safety and ethical tables) are explained rather than merely stated, showing thoughtful planning.
The procedure and materials list (with instrument uncertainties and photographs) allow reproducibility with only minor clarifications needed.
weaknesses: The methodology lacks justification for the chosen sample size (n=3) and omits control of temperature and agitation.
Ambiguities remain in the weighing protocol (shell only vs. shell plus solution), which could affect reproducibility.
Some minor context gaps (no reference to empirical eggshell dissolution data) prevent a fully exhaustive design description.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the student situates the research question in a specific and appropriate context and explains methodological considerations well (two strands at band 5), the methodology description contains a few ambiguities (band 4). On balance, the performance aligns with a solid 4/6 when adjusting down to meet the total-mark constraint.

strengths: Raw data tables are complete, clearly labelled, and processed data include appropriate significant figures.
Qualitative observations complement quantitative data, adding valuable contextual insight.
Data processing (means, standard deviations, t-test) is appropriate to the research question.
weaknesses: Uncertainties are quoted but not integrated into data tables or graphs; no error bars are present.
Grouping of pH categories lacks justification and a mis-quoted p-value undermines precision.
No propagation of uncertainty or justification for statistical assumptions (e.g., pooled variance) is provided.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Communication of data recording and processing is generally clear (band 4), but the major omissions in uncertainty consideration (band 2) and some inaccuracies in processing (band 3) position the overall criterion at a mid-band 3/6.

strengths: The conclusion explicitly states the statistical finding and ties back to the hypotheses.
Relevance to the research question is maintained throughout the conclusion.
weaknesses: Inconsistent reporting of the p-value and incorrect inequality interpretation undermine consistency.
Comparison to the accepted scientific context is superficial, lacking quantitative or mechanistic discussion and literature references.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the conclusion is relevant and mostly consistent with the analysis (band 4), the incorrect statistical interpretation and superficial scientific context comparison (band 2) yield an overall 2/6 for this criterion.

strengths: Specific methodological weaknesses (shell size variability, evaporation, carbonation) are identified and their impacts articulated.
Improvements proposed (standardised shells, container coverings, degassing, extended duration) are realistic and linked to identified limitations.
weaknesses: The relative impact of individual weaknesses is not prioritized or deeply explained.
Suggestions to improve statistical power (e.g., increasing n) and pH validation steps are missing.
Explanation depth does not fully meet the âexplainedâ descriptor for top-band improvements.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student describes specific weaknesses and relevant improvements (both at band 4) but stops short of explaining their relative impact or offering deeper methodological justification. This aligns the evaluation at 3/6 when scaled to fit the total-mark requirement.

strandSelections.reasoning: The conclusion explicitly states that variation in pH did not produce a statistically significant difference and correctly references the calculated p-value, tying back to H0/H1.  Minor inconsistencies (wrong inequality wording, reliance on broad groupings) prevent full alignment but **overall it remains relevant and largely consistent with the analysis**.

Raw data tables are complete, trials clearly labelled, units included, and a processed-data bar chart is provided.  Formatting is generally neat, though some tables are crowded and headings occasionally wrap awkwardly.  **The communication is clear (readers can follow values) and mostly precise (appropriate sig. figs in processed data), meeting the 3-4 descriptor.**

Mass loss, means, standard deviations and a two-sample t-test are carried out, all relevant to the question.  However, grouping sodas into only two pH categories is debatable, p-value mis-quoted once (0.447 vs 0.477), and no justification of pooled variance is given.  **Processing is appropriate but contains noticeable inaccuracies**, fitting the 3-4 band; a mid-band 3 is awarded.

Specific weaknessesâvariable shell size, evaporation, carbonationâare described, and their likely impact on demineralisation data is articulated.  The student does not prioritise which weakness is most influential, so the discussion stops short of the âexplains relative impactâ requirement for the top band, but **meets the descriptor for 3-4 marks.**

The student notes that qualitative observations appear to support accepted wisdom that greater acidity increases demineralisation, but gives no literature comparison, quantitative reference, or mechanistic discussion beyond general statements.  **This is only a superficial comparison to scientific context**, fitting the 1-2 band.

The student states the research question clearly and embeds it within a focused discussion of soda acidity, dental health, and the suitability of eggshells as a CaCO3 model.  Background paragraphs link the question to realâworld concerns (tooth decay) and cite literature values for beverage pH, demonstrating **specific and appropriate context rather than a broad or generic backdrop**.  Minor gaps (e.g. no explicit reference to previous empirical findings on eggshell dissolution rates) do not detract substantially from the contextualisation expected in the top band.

Instrument uncertainties for balance, timer and beaker are quoted, yet no uncertainty columns are shown in raw tables, no error bars appear in the graph, and no propagation is attempted.  **This is limited evidence of consideration with major omissions**, corresponding to the lower band (1-2).

Improvements (standardised shell cutting, covering containers, degassing sodas, extending duration) are realistic and clearly linked to the stated limitations, with brief explanations of how each would enhance reliability.  **They are described rather than fully explained in depth**, so the strand sits comfortably in the 3-4 band.

The design section identifies IV, DV and a control, gives a rationale for five pH levels plus water, and lists several relevant control variables with reasons and methods of control.  Safety, ethical and environmental tables further show awareness of data-collection issues.  **These considerations are explained, not merely stated**, and are sufficient to obtain data that address the question (three trials, 24-h monitoring).  Some omissions (no justification for only three trials; no mention of temperature) keep the strand just below a perfect 6.

A step-by-step procedure, materials list with instrument uncertainties, and photographs allow another investigator to reproduce the work with minor clarification (e.g. whether the whole shell-plus-liquid mass or shell alone is weighed; drying time applicability to every trial).  These few ambiguities align with the **3-4 band, as overall reproducibility is achievable but not flawless**.

annotations.comment: The demineralization description ends abruptly, leaving the reaction mechanism incomplete. Please complete the sentence and clarify how Hâº interacts with COâÂ²â» to form soluble products.
The null hypothesis omits âhoursâ after âover 24,â which could cause confusion. Revise it to read âover 24 hoursâ for precision.
The alternative hypothesis repeats elements of Hâ without clarifying directional effect. Consider specifying the expected direction of mass loss variation for clarity.
Methodology describes three trials but lacks justification for this sample size and omits temperature control. Explain why n=3 is sufficient and specify ambient temperature conditions.
Table 1 cites Reddy et al. and Whites twice in the caption; this is redundant. Consolidate references or choose the most relevant source to streamline citation practice.
While the dependent variable is defined, it is unclear whether the mass measurement refers solely to dry shells or includes solution. Specify the weighing protocol (e.g., shell-only after blotting).
Control variables are well listed, but temperature and agitation are not controlled. Consider adding constant temperature baths or stating that samples remain undisturbed throughout exposure.
The p-value is reported as 0.447 in Table 11 but later discussed as 0.477. Please reconcile these values to maintain consistency.

The research question is clearly situated in a specific context linking soda acidity, dental health concerns, and eggshell CaCOâ as a model. This focus enhances relevance and shows thoughtful contextualisation.
Personal engagement is well demonstrated through relevant anecdotal experience linked to academic inquiry. This establishes strong motivation and adds authenticity to the investigation.
Safety, environmental and ethical considerations are comprehensive, demonstrating strong awareness of practical risks and sustainability. Excellent inclusion of multiple factors.
The materials list includes tap water, yet no further use is described. Remove or clarify its role to avoid confusion in reproducibility.
Procedure is detailed but omits instructions to blot and dry shells prior to weighing. Include a step to standardize shell preparation before each mass measurement.
Raw data tables are complete, but no uncertainty columns are presented. Incorporate instrumental uncertainties into raw tables to improve data transparency.
The repetition of trials is clearly stated and ensures reliability through replication. This strengthens the experimental design.
Consistency in raw data formatting across Trials 2 and 3 is commendable, facilitating direct comparison and reducing reader confusion.
The qualitative observations section effectively complements quantitative data, offering valuable context such as foam formation and shell staining.
Simplified data tables lack units in column headings for mass loss. Include âgâ in headings to maintain clarity and consistency.
The note on foam production is insightful; consider quantifying foam volume or bubble count to correlate qualitative observations with reaction rate.
The standard deviation formula uses N in the denominator rather than Nâ1 for sample data. Justify the choice or adjust to the sample standard deviation formula.
Spriteâs large standard deviation (5.40 g) in Table 8 is not discussed. Address this variability by exploring potential outliers or procedural inconsistencies.
The bar chart is clear but needs error bars to reflect measured variability (standard deviations) and improve data communication precision.
The pH grouping criteria in Table 9 are not justified. Explain why the threshold was set at pH 3.10 and discuss implications of grouping.
Table 10 shows an extremely low standard deviation (0.007 g) for high-acid group, suggesting over-rounding. Clarify calculation steps and rounding rules.
The conclusion incorrectly states p < Î±, despite p > Î±. Revise the interpretation to reflect that the null hypothesis cannot be rejected.
Strengths of consistent surface area exposure and multiple time-point measurements are noted. To further enhance, discuss how these features improved statistical robustness.
Limitations discuss shell size variability and evaporation; missing is the impact of small sample size on statistical power. Consider adding commentary on increasing n.
Weaknesses table omits calibration or verification of pH values. Include a step to validate beverage pH with a calibrated meter for accuracy.
Bibliography entries vary in style and sometimes repeat information. Ensure consistent formatting (e.g., APA) and remove duplicate author listings.

strands.name: Conclusion relevance and consistency
strands.content: A conclusion is justified that is relevant to the research question and fully consistent with the analysis presented.

strands.name: Scientific context comparison

strands.name: Improvements
